title: Next Steps — Your UCF BIP Pathway
objectives: Schedule a 1:1 consultation with the UCF BIP team
Understand what your continued journey with UCF BIP offers beyond this program
Identify their top 3 priority actions for the next 30 days

contentBlocks.title: Your Next Move from Campus Headquarters
contentBlocks.content:
With your resources downloaded and your readiness scores in hand, you are standing at the front door of UCF's incubator complex. This is where the program stops being a course and starts being a relationship. One action matters most: schedule your 1:1 meeting with Brian Bedrick and the UCF BIP team.

What does this actually mean day to day? This is your orientation inside headquarters — the team gets to know your mission and helps you plan the route. It is a strategy session, not a sales call. A 30-minute conversation can save you months of trial and error.

What to Bring

Bring your readiness scores and your questions — that is your entry briefing for the team.

  • Your readiness self-assessment scores from Lesson 8-1
  • Your completed Expansion Decision Worksheet from Lesson 8-2
  • Your top 3 questions: written down before the meeting
  • Your business context : company, product, home market, and why you're considering the U.S.

Book Your Meeting Now

BOOK YOUR 1:1 MEETING

Free, low-pressure, and the natural next step.

contentBlocks.title: Founder at UCF 1:1 Meeting
contentBlocks.alt: Illustration of a founder shaking hands with a UCF business advisor across a desk in a warmly lit campus office, with a printed action plan, laptop, and coffee cups on the table.

contentBlocks.title: Did You Know?
contentBlocks.content: UCF is consistently ranked among the top universities in the nation for producing graduates ready to enter the workforce, and its proximity to major employers like Lockheed Martin, Siemens, and the space industry at nearby Cape Canaveral creates a unique talent pipeline. The Central Florida Research Park, adjacent to campus, is one of the largest research parks in the country, giving incubator companies access to specialized talent and research partnerships that would be difficult to find elsewhere.

contentBlocks.title: What Comes After Phase 1
contentBlocks.content:
Phase 1 gave you the map. Your continued journey with UCF BIP puts you on the road. This program is Phase 1: the foundation before spending money in the U.S. market. Some founders move forward independently. Others benefit from the next stages of the UCF BIP market entry/soft landing program:

  • Personalized advisory . One-on-one guidance specific to your business
  • Professional connections . Warm introductions to attorneys, CPAs, and banking contacts
  • Ecosystem access . Investors, industry contacts, and partner organizations
  • Peer community . Other international founders at a similar stage
  • Structured milestones . Progress tracking with regular check-ins

Details are discussed during your 1:1 meeting. Your conversation will help determine which path makes sense.

contentBlocks.title: Your Top 3 Actions for the Next 30 Days
contentBlocks.content:
These are your first three moves after leaving campus. Pick your top three actions for the next 30 days. Three, not ten. Specific actions lead to higher follow-through than long lists.

Make Each Action Specific

Here's how this plays out. Weak: "Learn more about entity types." Strong: "Schedule a consultation with a business attorney to discuss LLC vs. C-Corp for my visa situation by [date]."

High-impact 30-day actions:

  1. Book your 1:1 with UCF BIP . Do it today.
  2. Contact a specific professional . Attorney, CPA, or immigration lawyer. "Email this referral by Friday."
  3. Complete one research task . SunBiz name check, USPTO search, or compile bank documentation.
  4. Register for a free resource . SBDC counseling, SCORE mentorship, or a chamber event.
  5. Complete the Expansion Decision Worksheet . Block 60 minutes before your 1:1.

contentBlocks.title: Write Down Your Top 3 Actions
contentBlocks.instructions:
Complete this exercise right now — do not save it for later. Write down your three highest-priority actions for the next 30 days. For each one, include:

  1. The specific action . What exactly will you do?
  2. The deadline . By what date will you complete it?
  3. The first step . What is the very first thing you need to do to start? (An email to send, a website to visit, a phone call to make.)

Action 1: _______________________________________________

Deadline: ________ | First step: ___________________________

Action 2: _______________________________________________

Deadline: ________ | First step: ___________________________

Action 3: _______________________________________________

Deadline: ________ | First step: ___________________________

If your first action is not "Book my 1:1 with UCF BIP," reconsider. That meeting is free, it takes 30 minutes, and it gives you a sounding board for everything else on your list.

contentBlocks.title: Important Reminder Before You Go
contentBlocks.content:
This program provides general educational information to help international founders understand the U.S. business environment. It does not constitute legal, tax, immigration, or financial advice. Founders should consult qualified professionals before making business decisions.

This is not a formality. It is genuinely important. The information in this program gives you a strong foundation, but every founder's case is different. Your entity choice, your tax duties, your visa pathway, your IP strategy: all of these depend on your specific case. The experts you work with will take the general knowledge you gained here and tailor it to your particular business, your country of origin, your industry, and your goals. That is how knowledge becomes safe, real action.

contentBlocks.title: One Last Thing — Your Feedback Matters
contentBlocks.content:
Now, before you close this program, two questions. Your answers help make this better for every founder after you.

Was This Program Worth Your Time?

Did it change your understanding of U.S. market entry? Help you avoid at least one costly mistake? Be honest. Positive and critical answers help equally.

What Would Make It Better?

A topic in more depth? An area too basic? A resource you wished existed? Share your thoughts during your 1:1 or send them to the UCF BIP team.

tabs.label: Skills Gained
tabs.content:
**By completing this course, you've developed practical knowledge in:**

- U.S. business entity selection and registration
- Federal, state, and local tax compliance
- Intellectual property protection strategies
- Hiring and visa pathway planning
- U.S. market entry and customer acquisition
- Cross-cultural business communication
- Central Florida ecosystem navigation

tabs.label: Your Credential
tabs.content:
**POK Tech Digital Credential**

Your completion of this program earns you a verified digital credential from POK Tech.

**What this means:**
- Shareable on LinkedIn and professional profiles
- Verifiable by employers and partners
- Demonstrates commitment to professional development
- Recognized by UCF BIP program for Phase 2 eligibility

tabs.label: What's Next
tabs.content:
**This is the beginning, not the end.**

1. **Apply for UCF BIP Phase 2** — hands-on mentorship and market validation
2. **Book your SBDC consultation** — get personalized business planning support
3. **Connect with fellow founders** — join the UCF international entrepreneur network
4. **Take action this week** — choose ONE thing from this course and do it today

**Your U.S. business journey starts now.**

questions.stem: A founder has completed Phase 1 (this program), landed her first two U.S. customers, and generated early revenue. Which UCF BIP phase is designed for her current business stage?
options: Phase 1. She should retake the program to reinforce the fundamentals
Phase 2, designed for founders establishing initial U.S. operations with a validated opportunity
Phase 3, designed for founders with early traction (customers and revenue) who need help scaling
She has outgrown the UCF BIP program and should work independently
questions.explanation: Phase 3 of the UCF BIP market entry/soft landing program is specifically designed for founders who already have initial traction : early customers, revenue, or signed partnerships. It focuses on growth strategy, investor connections, expanded networking, and operational support for scaling. Phase 2 is for founders still establishing initial operations.

questions.stem: According to the lesson's guidance on effective goal-setting, which of the following is the STRONGEST action item for a founder's 30-day plan?
options: Schedule a 30-minute consultation with a CPA who works with foreign-owned businesses by March 28
Research the market and figure out pricing
Get everything set up as soon as possible
questions.explanation: Effective action items must be specific, time-bound, and completable. 'Schedule a 30-minute consultation with a CPA who works with foreign-owned businesses by March 28' includes a specific action (schedule consultation), a specific qualifier (CPA with international experience), and a deadline. Vague goals like 'learn more' or 'research the market' lack the specificity needed for follow-through.

questions.stem: A founder from Guatemala has completed this Phase 1 program and wants to take action this week. She has not yet formed a U.S. entity, has no SBDC appointment, and has not completed the Expansion Decision Worksheet. Based on the lesson's guidance on prioritizing next steps, which specific set of actions should she take in the next seven days?
options: Immediately file an LLC on SunBiz and open a bank account so she can start accepting U.S. payments
Focus entirely on attending networking events to build connections before doing any administrative work
Wait until she feels fully confident in all seven module topics before taking any action
Book her 1:1 meeting with UCF BIP, schedule a free SBDC counseling session at floridasbdc.org, and block 60 minutes to complete the Expansion Decision Worksheet before the meeting
questions.explanation: The lesson emphasizes three specific first-week actions: book the UCF BIP 1:1 meeting (free, 30 minutes, the single most important next step), connect with a support resource like SBDC, and complete the Expansion Decision Worksheet so the BIP team can give targeted advice. Filing an LLC before consulting a professional risks choosing the wrong entity type. Waiting for full confidence delays progress unnecessarily. Networking matters, but structured support should come first.

contentBlocks.title: Key Takeaway
contentBlocks.content: The door is open. The next move is yours. You have arrived at campus headquarters not as a finish line, but as a starting point. You have the knowledge, the resources, and the access to a team that wants to help you succeed. The founders who tend to get the most value from this program are the ones who take action within the first week. Apply what you learned: book your 1:1 meeting, then create your top 3 actions with specific deadlines. Take the first step today. Central Florida is ready for you, and the UCF BIP team is ready to help you get there.
